skill.name: godot-4-migration
skill.description: Specialized guide for migrating Godot 3.x projects to Godot 4 (GDScript 2.0), covering syntax changes, Tweens, and exports.
skill.summary: Guide for migrating Godot 3.x projects to Godot 4 with GDScript 2.0 syntax changes.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The skill contains only documentation for Godot 4 migration with GDScript code examples. No shell commands, cryptographic code, or system reconnaissance present.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: Shell Command Detection
medium_findings.description: Scanner flagged GDScript method calls like create_tween() and $Tween as shell commands. These are Godot game engine API calls, not system commands.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: All flagged lines contain GDScript code examples (e.g., $Tween.interpolate_property, create_tween()) which are Godot API methods, not shell execution

medium_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
medium_findings.description: Scanner triggered on word 'deprecated' which contains character patterns matching crypto detection. No cryptographic code exists in this skill.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The word 'deprecated' triggered false pattern match. No cryptographic algorithms present.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
medium_findings.description: Scanner triggered on the word 'Problem:' in troubleshooting section. This is a documentation heading, not reconnaissance.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line contains 'Problem:' as a documentation heading in troubleshooting section.

content.value_statement: Game developers upgrading Godot projects face syntax errors and deprecated features. This skill provides a comprehensive guide for migrating GDScript 2.0 code with concrete examples.
actual_capabilities: Translate Godot 3 export variables to @export annotations
Convert deprecated Tween node usage to create_tween()
Migrate signal connections from string-based to callable syntax
Update yield() calls to await syntax
Apply typed array syntax for better performance
Replace setter/getter patterns with inline property syntax
limitations: Does not automatically convert project files
Cannot migrate custom C# or GDExtension code
Does not handle editor settings migration
No support for downgrading Godot 4 projects to Godot 3

use_cases.title: Port Game Projects
use_cases.description: Systematically upgrade entire game projects from Godot 3.x to Godot 4 with step-by-step syntax guidance.
use_cases.target_user: Game developers with existing Godot 3 projects

use_cases.title: Fix Syntax Errors
use_cases.description: Quickly resolve compilation errors when opening Godot 3 projects in Godot 4.

use_cases.title: Learn GDScript 2.0
use_cases.description: Understand the new features and patterns in GDScript 2.0 for new Godot 4 development.
use_cases.target_user: New Godot 4 developers learning the language

prompt_templates.title: Basic Migration Help
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I convert an @export var in Godot 4? Show me the old and new syntax.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick reference for export annotation changes

prompt_templates.title: Tween System Migration
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this Godot 3 Tween code to Godot 4: $Tween.interpolate_property($Sprite, 'position', Vector2(0,0), Vector2(100,100), 1.0, 0, 0)

prompt_templates.title: Signal Connection Update
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I connect signals using callables in Godot 4 instead of connect('signal_name', self, '_handler')?

prompt_templates.title: Full Project Assessment
prompt_templates.prompt: List all the major syntax changes needed to migrate a Godot 3 project to Godot 4, with examples for each category.

output_examples.input: Convert this Godot 3 code to Godot 4: export var speed = 10
output_examples.output:
In Godot 4, use the @export annotation:

```gdscript
@export var speed: int = 10
```

You can also add type hints and inspector hints:
```gdscript
@export_range(1, 100) var speed: int = 10
```

output_examples.input: How do I use Tweens in Godot 4?
output_examples.output:
The Tween node is deprecated. Use create_tween() method:

```gdscript
var tween = create_tween()
tween.tween_property($Sprite, 'position', Vector2(100, 100), 1.0)
tween.parallel().tween_property($Sprite, 'modulate:a', 0.0, 1.0)
```

best_practices: Use @export_range, @export_file, and other @export annotations for better editor integration
Type all variables with explicit type hints for performance gains in GDScript 2.0
Use super() to call parent methods instead of the old .method_name() syntax
anti_patterns: Do not use emit_signal('name') when you can use name.emit() with the signal object
Do not use string-based signal connections like connect('pressed', self, '_handler')
Do not use yield() for coroutines - use await instead in Godot 4

faq.question: Does this skill automatically convert my project files?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides guidance and examples. The actual conversion must be done manually or using Godot's built-in migration tools.

faq.question: Can I migrate C# code with this skill?
faq.answer: No, this skill covers GDScript syntax only. C# migration requires separate considerations.

faq.question: What is the main difference between GDScript 1.0 and 2.0?
faq.answer: GDScript 2.0 adds type hints, new annotation system (@export, @onready), inline setters/getters, and replaces yield with await.

faq.question: How do I handle onready variables in Godot 4?
faq.answer: Use @onready annotation: @onready var sprite = $Sprite instead of onready var sprite = $Sprite

faq.question: Is Tween node still available in Godot 4?
faq.answer: The Tween node is deprecated. Use create_tween() method in your script to create Tweens programmatically.

faq.question: Can I use Godot 3 assets in Godot 4?
faq.answer: Most assets work, but some may need updating. Textures and meshes generally work, but scripts must be migrated to GDScript 2.0.
